data.title: Faraday Future Founder and Co-CEO YT Jia Shares Weekly Investor Update: FF to Establish the First Scaled EAI Education System in the United States With Deployment of Its EAI Robotics Products and Technology
data.description:
Faraday Future Founder and Co-CEO YT Jia Shares Weekly Investor Update: FF to Establish the First Scaled EAI Education System in the United States With Deployment of Its EAI Robotics Products and Technology

  • 2025 annual financial report showed that the Company’s stockholders’ equity turned from negative to positive.
  • The EAI robotics business achieved a positive gross margin on products in its very first quarter of delivery this year and has set a target of over 1,000 units shipment by the end of 2026.

Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. (NASDAQ: FFAI) (“Faraday Future”, “FF” or the “Company”), a California-based global Embodied AI (EAI) ecosystem company, today shared a weekly business update from YT Jia, Founder and Global Co-CEO of FF. This week’s update will be in the form of a Q&A session with YT.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260405923626/en/

Faraday Future Founder and Co-CEO YT Jia Shares Weekly Investor Update: FF to Establish the First Scaled EAI Education System in the United States with Deployment of its EAI Robotics Products and Technology

“Q1: YT, you mentioned that, in your opinion, the most important highlight of this annual financial report is that the Company’s net equity turned from negative to positive. What happened behind the scenes? Why is it so important?

A1: Actually, there’s quite a story behind this. Last year, two days before the release of our third-quarter financial report, we were dealt with a severe blow. Based on what we believe were extremely conservative accounting principles, the audit firm recorded a one-time, large impairment of approximately $130 million related to equipment and tooling for the FF 91, given the lower-than-expected utilization in the near term. This amount accounted for nearly two-thirds of the Company’s total loss for the period and directly contributing to the Company’s net equity turning negative, as well as a two-day delay in the release of the third-quarter financial report. At the time, in order to file the financial report, we had no choice but to accept this outcome. We were deeply frustrated and we sympathize with our investors about that.

After that, we conducted deep internal reflection and quickly initiated debt optimization and a series of remedial measures. Through ongoing communications with creditors like suppliers, the company gained their understanding for its strategic direction. The company successfully completed approximately $100 million in debt optimization and reduction, which made restoring net equity to a positive position possible. We are truly grateful for their support.

Q2: The EAI robotics business achieved a positive gross margin on products in its very first quarter of delivery and has set a target of over 1,000 units shipment by the end of 2026. What does this mean for the company overall? In which areas will you focus on your main efforts?

A2: This is the first time since FF was founded that we achieved positive product gross margin on products, which is indeed a significant milestone. First, going from zero to one is critical, as it validates the feasibility of FF EAI’s business model of ‘Launch is sales, sales is delivery, and delivery equals positive gross margin.’ Second, it brings us closer to achieving positive operating cash flow more quickly and ultimately moving toward profitability. This is also extremely helpful for the financing initiatives we are currently advancing. Third, FF’s unique Online Direct Sales and Co-creation Ecosystem is proving to be a powerful driver in accelerating the execution of our strategic goals. Fourth, it is accelerating the rollout of the ‘Device revenue + Skills revenue + Data revenue’ ecosystem-based revenue model, while continuously amplifying our ‘Device–Data–Brain’ flywheel effect as the first company in the U.S. to deliver both humanoid and biomimetic robots. Our goal is to replicate, in the EAI robotics industry, the same kind of flywheel effect that Tesla has achieved across EVs, data, and FSD.

Moving forward, to meet this year’s shipment target of over 1,000 units as soon as possible, we will implement the ‘6-3-3’ strategy in phases and step-by-step. The core scenarios of the first phase will be divided into two main areas: B2B and B2C. We have already provided a systematic introduction to the key B2B use cases before, so I will not go into detail again today. On the B2C side, family education will become our first core use case, with a strong focus on education, learning, and growth companionship in the AI era, helping children become leaders of the AI age. We have already started discussions with relevant departments of the California government. Starting from California, we hope to work together with school districts to build AI robotics labs across K–12 education and the UC public university system, including UCLA and UC Berkeley, and to establish the first scaled EAI education system in the United States.

Q3: You mentioned Tesla. Recent commentary suggests that revenue from FSD and robotics is expected to account for more than half of the company’s total revenue for the first time in 2027. What is your view on that?

A3: That is a very good question. We believe that competition among technology companies, both today and in the future, will be a competition of AI, data, and ecosystem capabilities. We believe that whoever moves first in completing the transition from simply ‘selling hardware’ to ‘selling AI, services, data, and ecosystem value’ will emerge as the winner and help define the landscape of the industry in the next generation. At the same time, from the perspective of industry scale, EAI industry will eventually surpass the automotive industry in total output value. Perhaps one day in the future, all 7 billion people in the world will have their own EAI agent. If and when that happens, this industry will enter a period of explosive growth very quickly.

Q4: That brings us to your son. He was recently admitted to Stanford’s CS program, and the news even became a top trending topic on social media. A lot of people are curious—how did he get into so many world-class universities? Could you share your formula with other parents?

A4: I wouldn’t say there’s any magic formula. To be honest, I actually feel a little guilty, because over the years I have done relatively little to support my children’s growth, especially in daily life. He earned offers from many top universities through his own hard work and outstanding performance. If there is anything I may have contributed, it is probably this: from a very early age, he became genuinely interested in computers and AI, and he found his direction early. That matters a lot. More importantly, throughout his K–12 years, he tried to spend every school break interning at AI companies like FF whenever he could. Those experiences gave him a much clearer understanding of what he wanted to learn and why it mattered, while also helping him build meaningful hands-on experience in AI. I believe that was likely one of the factors that helped him earn admission to schools like Stanford.

Very soon, I plan to purchase several FF robots for my children to use for learning, secondary development projects for universities, and exploration in K–12 education settings. I hope our experience can offer some useful inspiration to parents. This is also why we have chosen education and growth companionship as the first core use case in our initial phase. We hope to create more AI and EAI-powered learning environments and hands-on opportunities for parents and children, both at school and at home. That is all for today. Next week, I will take questions on Super One, capital markets, and our upgraded Ten-Punch Combo. Stay tuned.”

ABOUT FARADAY FUTURE

Faraday Future is a California-based global intelligent Company founded in 2014 and is dedicated to reshaping the future of mobility through vehicle electrification, intelligent technologies, and AI innovation. Its flagship vehicle, the FF 91, began deliveries in 2023 and reflects the brand’s pursuit of ultra-luxury, cutting-edge technology, and high performance. FF’s second brand, FX, targets the high-volume mainstream vehicle market. Its first model, Super One, is positioned as a first-class EAI-MPV, with deliveries planned to begin in 2026. FF recently announced its entry into the Embodied AI Robotics business with sales beginning this year, connecting its future strategy of bringing a new era of EAI vehicles and EAI robotics. For more information, please visit https://www.ff.com/

FORWARD LOOKING STATEMENTS

This press release includes “forward looking statements” within the meaning of the safe harbor provisions of the United States Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. When used in this press release, the words “plan to,” “can,” “will,” “should,” “future,” “potential,” and variations of these words or similar expressions (or the negative versions of such words or expressions) are intended to identify forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements, which include statements regarding potential future legal actions against alleged illegal market manipulation or similar improper activities, and FF’s entry into the embodied AI robotics market and robotics deliveries and development, involve a number of known and unknown risks, uncertainties, assumptions and other important factors, many of which are outside the Company’s control, which could cause actual results or outcomes to differ materially from those discussed in the forward-looking statements. Important factors, that may affect actual results or outcomes include, among others: the Company’s ability to timely regain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid requirement; the Company’s common stock will be suspended from trading on Nasdaq if its closing price is $0.10 or less for 10 consecutive trading days; the Company’s ability to continue as a going concern and improve its liquidity and financial position; the Company’s ability to pay its outstanding obligations, which it currently lacks; the availability of sufficient share capital to meet its current obligations and execute on its strategy, which the Company currently lacks; the agreement of stockholders to substantially increase the Company’s share capital, which could result in substantial additional dilution; the willingness of convertible debt investors to fund the Company while it lacks sufficient share capital for conversions; demand for the Company’s robotics products; the ability of B2B preorder companies to locate customers to purchase our robotics products, on which their nonbinding preorders substantially depend; competition in the robotics industry, which includes companies with far superior experience, funding and name recognition; the Company’s reliance on a single OEM for most of its robotics products; the Company’s ability to get the planned robotics products to comply with all applicable U.S. rules and regulations; the ability of the robotics OEM to timely supply robotics to the Company; tariff uncertainty for imported products, particularly from China; demand from automobile dealers for robotics products; the Company’s ability to homologate FX vehicles for sale; the Company’s ability to secure the necessary funding to execute on the FX strategy, which is substantial; the Company’s ability to secure an occupancy certificate covering all of its Hanford facility; the Company’s ability to remediate its material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting and the risks related to the restatement of previously issued consolidated financial statements; the Company’s limited operating history and the significant barriers to growth it faces; the Company’s history of substantial losses and expectation of continued losses; the success of the Company’s payroll expense reduction plan; the Company’s ability to execute on its plans to develop and market its vehicles and the timing of these development programs; the Company’s estimates of the size of the markets for its vehicles and cost to bring those vehicles to market; the rate and degree of market acceptance of the Company’s vehicles; the Company’s ability to cover future warranty claims; the success of other competing manufacturers; the performance and security of the Company’s vehicles; current and potential litigation involving the Company; the Company’s ability to receive funds from, satisfy the conditions precedent of and close on the various financings described elsewhere by the Company; the result of future financing efforts, the failure of any of which could result in the Company seeking protection under the Bankruptcy Code; the Company’s indebtedness; the Company’s ability to use its “at-the-market” program; insurance coverage; general economic and market conditions impacting demand for the Company’s products; potential negative impacts of a reverse stock split; potential cost, headcount and salary reduction actions may not be sufficient or may not achieve their expected results; circumstances outside of the Company’s control, such as natural disasters, climate change, health epidemics and pandemics, terrorist attacks, and civil unrest; risks related to the Company’s operations in China; the success of the Company’s remedial measures taken in response to the Special Committee findings; the Company’s dependence on its suppliers and contract manufacturer; the Company’s ability to develop and protect its technologies; the Company’s ability to protect against cybersecurity risks; and the ability of the Company to attract and retain employees, any adverse developments in existing legal proceedings or the initiation of new legal proceedings, and volatility of the Company’s stock price. You should carefully consider the foregoing factors and the other risks and uncertainties described in the “Risk Factors” section of the Company’s Form 10-K filed with the SEC on March 31, 2025, and Form 10-Qs for the quarters ended June 30, 2025 and September 30, 2025 filed with the SEC on May 9, 2025, August 19, 2025 and November 21, 2025, respectively, and other documents filed by the Company from time to time with the SEC.

PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) Investors: April 20, 2026, Deadline in Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit

PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) Investors: April 20, 2026, Deadline in Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit

PR Newswire

RADNOR, Pa., April 5, 2026

Did you buy PYPL common stock between February 8, 2024, and February 2, 2026?

Affected PayPal Holdings, Inc. Investor Summary

  • Who: PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL)
  • What: Securities fraud class action lawsuits filed
  • Class Period: February 8, 2024, through February 2, 2026
  • Deadline to Seek Lead Plaintiff Status: April 20, 2026
  • Key Lawsuit Allegations: Material misstatements and/or omissions concerning the company's projected revenue outlook and anticipated growth.
  • Investor Action: Contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (www.ktmc.com) for recovery options at no cost to investor

RADNOR, Pa., April 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (www.ktmc.com), a nationally recognized securities litigation law firm, informs investors that securities fraud class action lawsuits have been filed against PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PayPal) (NASDAQ: PYPL) on behalf of those who purchased or acquired PayPal common stock between February 8, 2024, and February 2, 2026, inclusive. Investors have until April 20, 2026, to file for lead plaintiff status. 

CONTACT KTMC TO DISCUSS YOUR LEGAL RIGHTS:
If you purchased or acquired PayPal common stock and have lost money on your investment, you are encouraged to contact KTMC attorney Jonathan Naji, Esq. at:

(484) 270-1453
info@ktmc.com
https://www.ktmc.com/pypl-paypal-holdings-inc-class-action-lawsuit?utm_source=PR_Newswire&utm_medium=pressrelease&utm_campaign=pypl&mktm=PR 

There is no cost or obligation to speak with an attorney.

PAYPAL HOLDINGS, INC. CLASS ACTION LAWSUITS - COMPLAINTS ALLEGATION SUMMARY: 
The complaints allege that, throughout the Class Period, Defendants made materially false and/or misleading statements, as well as failed to disclose material adverse facts about PayPal's business and operations.  Specifically, Defendants created the false impression that they possessed reliable information pertaining to PayPal's projected revenue outlook and anticipated growth while also minimizing risk from seasonality and macroeconomic fluctuations. In truth, PayPal's optimistic plan for growth through various initiatives to bolster PayPal's Branded Checkout offerings fell short of reality as the 2027 targets were not achievable under the tenure of PayPal's CEO and required both an unrealistically stable consumer landscape and strong execution with clear direction from PayPal and its management.  

Why did PayPal's Stock Drop?

On February 3, 2026, PayPal announced a surprise leadership change replacing the company's CEO. The leadership change coincided with PayPal's fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings report, wherein PayPal missed consensus estimates for both revenue and profit.   On this news, PayPal's stock price fell $10.63, or 20.3%, to close at $41.70 per share on February 3, 2026. 

WHAT PYPL INVESTORS CAN DO NOW:

  1. File to be lead plaintiff by April 20, 2026.
  2. Contact KTMC for a free case evaluation. All representation is on a contingency fee basis, there is no cost to you.
  3. Retain counsel of choice or take no action.

THE LEAD PLAINTIFF PROCESS FOR PAYPAL HOLDINGS, INC. INVESTORS:
PayPal investors may, no later than April 20, 2026, seek to be appointed as a lead plaintiff representative of the class through Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP or other counsel, or may choose to do nothing and remain an absent class member. A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of all class members in directing the litigation.  The lead plaintiff is usually the investor or small group of investors who have the largest financial interest and who are also adequate and typical of the proposed class of investors. The lead plaintiff selects counsel to represent the lead plaintiff and the class and these attorneys, if approved by the court, are lead or class counsel. Your ability to share in any recovery is not affected by the decision of whether or not to serve as a lead plaintiff.

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP encourages PayPal investors to contact the firm for more information.

ABOUT KESSLER TOPAZ MELTZER & CHECK, LLP (KTMC):
Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (KTMC) is a leading U.S. plaintiff-side law firm focused on securities-fraud class actions and global investor protection. The firm represents individual investors as well as institutions, such as major pension funds, asset managers, and international investors. KTMC has led some of the largest recoveries in securities litigation and has been recognized by peers and the legal media with numerous accolades, including The National Law Journal's Plaintiff's Hot List and Trailblazers in Plaintiffs' Law, BTI Consulting Group's Honor Roll of Most Feared Law Firms, The Legal Intelligencer's Class Action Firm of the Year, Lawdragon's Leading Plaintiff Financial Lawyers, and Law360's Titans of the Plaintiffs Bar.  The firm operates globally with offices in Pennsylvania and California.  KTMC has recovered over $25 billion for our clients and the classes they represent.  For more information about Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP, please visit www.ktmc.com.  The complaints in this matter were not filed by KTMC.

data.title: TEAMSTERS REACH STRONG SETTLEMENT WITH UPS ON DRIVER SEVERANCE PACKAGES
data.description:
TEAMSTERS REACH STRONG SETTLEMENT WITH UPS ON DRIVER SEVERANCE PACKAGES

TEAMSTERS REACH STRONG SETTLEMENT WITH UPS ON DRIVER SEVERANCE PACKAGES

PR Newswire

WASHINGTON, April 5, 2026

O'Brien Pushes UPS to Negotiate on $150,000 Agreements for 7,500 Drivers Nationwide

WASHINGTON, April 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- After months of urging United Parcel Service (UPS) to honor its Teamsters contract and return to the bargaining table over unilateral driver buyouts, UPS on Sunday agreed to terms on a new settlement with the Teamsters National Negotiating Committee — capping severance offers while rewarding and protecting the seniority of Teamsters drivers.

Teamsters General President Sean M. O'Brien successfully pushed UPS into negotiations in the wake of national grievances filed against the package giant over its Driver Choice Program (DCP), which UPS pursued in February without agreement from the union. Those grievances forced UPS to withdraw the DCP in 13 states in March before ultimately agreeing to negotiate.

Under terms of the strong new settlement, UPS will be limited in the number of severance packages it can offer. Those who wish to accept them will receive $150,000 payments for early retirement. Offers will be made to long-haul feeder drivers and Regular Package Car Drivers based on seniority in all regions of the country. UPS has agreed not to pursue or offer any other severance programs for the life of the current Teamsters National Master Agreement, which does not expire until July 31, 2028.

"UPS never had the contractual right to unilaterally offer driver buyouts, but with enough pressure and member solidarity UPS finally did the right thing by putting its commitments to hardworking Teamsters down in writing," O'Brien said. "Lifelong Teamsters who have given so much of themselves to making UPS the king of parcel delivery will have the right of first refusal on any severance agreements. Union seniority and the rights of all our members will be honored. UPS will no longer have the chance to go around the union without giving Teamsters the respect they deserve at the bargaining table."

The settlement between the Teamsters and UPS caps the total number of severance payments to 7,500 drivers across all job classifications nationwide.

"Rank-and-file Teamsters have given their blood, sweat, tears and, most importantly, their labor to ensure UPS's success for a century. This agreement is a powerful reminder to UPS executives that the Teamsters never want to harm the company's ability to be competitive, but we will also never tolerate any violations of our contract or our members' rights," O'Brien said.

Founded in 1903, the International Brotherhood of Teamsters represents 1.3 million hardworking people in the U.S., Canada, and Puerto Rico. Visit Teamster.org for more information. Follow us on X @Teamsters and on Facebook at Facebook.com/teamsters.

Contact:
Kara Deniz, (202) 497-6610 
kdeniz@teamster.org

data.title: Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: U.S. Inflation Data -2-
data.description:
A run of low inflation buys the RBI some time before it has to move on rates, said Shilan Shah, economist at Capital Economics.

"The pressure to tighten policy over the coming weeks and months will build if the Middle East conflict intensifies and in particular if the rupee comes under further downward pressure," Shah said. 




  Taiwan 



  Taiwan is set to report March inflation data on Wednesday, which will likely show early signs of price pressures from the energy supply shock. 



  Most banks expect consumer inflation to remain under the 2% target watched by the central bank, but see mounting risks ahead. 



  Given the mix of likely higher inflation and still-solid economic growth, economists reckon the central bank could make a hawkish shift later in the year. 



  Still, unless inflation significantly overshoots expectations in the coming months, policymakers will likely stay on hold when they next meet in June, ING economists said. 



  Taiwan also releases its March trade data on Friday. Exports likely remained strong during the month thanks to the AI-related demand despite the Middle East conflict. Economists' estimates for export growth range from 20.3% to 35.5%. 





  Philippines, Thailand 



  The Philippines and Thailand are due to release inflation data for March, shedding light on how consumer prices in Southeast Asia are being influenced by the energy turmoil. 



  Both countries are particularly exposed to rising oil prices, given their heavy reliance on energy supplies from the Middle East. 



  The Philippine central bank expects inflation in March at 3.1%-3.9%. Inflation risks have intensified, fanned by the significant increase in domestic petroleum prices and higher rice prices, said Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas. 



  DBS's economics team expects the surge in energy prices to have snapped Thailand's deflationary streak in March, noting the sharp rise in domestic fuel prices after the government removed price caps. 





  Write to Jessica Fleetham at jessica.fleetham@wsj.com and Fabiana Negrin Ochoa at fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 05, 2026 17:14 ET (21:14 GMT)

data.title: Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: U.S. Inflation Data Will Be Watched Alongside Middle East Developments
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires staff 




  Below are the most important global economic events likely to affect FX and bond markets in the week starting April 6. 



  Developments with the war in the Middle East will remain at the center of investors' minds as heightened uncertainties remain over when the war will end. 



  U.S. inflation data for March will be closely watched amid concerns about how high energy costs will lift prices more broadly and the potential for this to result in interest-rate rises, as will minutes of the most recent Federal Reserve meeting. 



  "The Iranian conflict continues to serve as the main driver of global financial markets, with sentiment yo-yoing as expectations swing between anticipating a relatively short end to the war and more intensive and prolonged attacks," Investec economist Philip Shaw said in a note. 



  European and U.K. economic data will be somewhat sparse as most markets are closed on Monday for the Easter holiday. 



  In Asia, a round of inflation data from China and economies in Southeast Asia will be scrutinized for signs of stress caused by the energy market turmoil. Central banks in India, South Korea and New Zealand will deliver decisions as policymakers seek to contain the economic fallout of the war. 





  U.S. 



  Investors will be watching U.S. inflation data for March on Friday closely as they try to glean some idea of how the spike in energy prices due to the Middle East war has fed through into broader inflation. 



  This could give clues as to whether or not the Federal Reserve is likely to raise interest rates to tackle rising inflation. Fed minutes, due on Wednesday, will also be closely watched for signals on the likelihood of rate hikes. U.S. money markets currently price in unchanged rates in the months ahead, with a 27% chance of a rate reduction by the end of 2026, LSEG data showed. 



  "The upcoming [U.S.] CPI reading for March will show the initial impact of soaring energy markets, even if the U.S. is somewhat insulated by being a net exporter of oil and gas," AJ Bell analysts said in a note. "Close attention is likely to be paid to the core number, which strips out volatile food and energy costs, to get an idea of whether the inflation bug is spreading more broadly across the economy." 



  The Fed kept interest rates unchanged at its last meeting, and the minutes will shed light on how the decision was made and what policymakers are thinking, they said. 



  Other data will be watched for signs of how the U.S. economy is performing during the Middle East war. The ISM survey on services for March is due Monday, weekly jobless claims Thursday, while the University of Michigan's preliminary consumer survey for April is released Friday. 



  Also due are durable goods figures for February on Tuesday, followed by the third estimate of fourth-quarter gross domestic product and PCE inflation data for February on Thursday. 



  The U.S. Treasury will auction three-year notes on Tuesday, 10-year notes on Wednesday and 30-year bonds on Thursday. 





  Canada 



  Canadian jobs data for March are released on Friday. Investors will be watching these figures to gauge how the economy has fared since the start of the war in the Middle East and the consequent spike in energy prices. 





  Eurozone 



  Final March services purchasing managers' data for Spain, Italy, France, Germany and the eurozone are scheduled for Tuesday. These will be the first releases in a short week after the Easter holidays. Germany will publish manufacturing orders data for February on Wednesday, followed by German and Spanish industrial production data for February on Thursday and Italian industrial production data on Friday. 



  Final German inflation data for March are due on Friday. 



  Germany will sell February 2035- and August 2050-dated green Bunds on Tuesday and February 2036-dated conventional Bunds on Wednesday. Austria will hold a bond auction on Tuesday, Portugal on Wednesday, Spain on Thursday and Italy on Friday. 





  U.K. 



  Economic data releases in the U.K. are relatively thin on the ground, with markets closed on Monday for Easter. 



  The final reading of the U.K. purchasing managers' survey on services activity for March is released on Tuesday. 



  The RICS U.K. house-price survey for March on Thursday will likely be of interest to investors given that investors have priced in a risk of interest-rate hikes in the coming months, having previously priced in rate cuts. With that, swap rates and mortgage rates have risen, "which might have put a dampener on buyer demand for house purchases," said Investec economist Sandra Horsfield in a note. 





  Scandinavia 



  Norwegian inflation data for March will be released on Friday. Denmark and Sweden will hold bond auctions on Wednesday. 





  Switzerland 



  Switzerland will hold a bond auction on Wednesday. 





  China 



  China's first full inflation print since the war began is in the spotlight on Friday as markets watch to gauge the impact of the surge in energy prices. 



  A WSJ poll projects that consumer price growth held at 1.3% on the year last month, while producer prices finally swung back into the green after more than three years in negative territory. Economists tip PPI at 0.5% in March versus -0.9% in February. 



  Markets will also monitor local gasoline price moves, ING economists said, as the biweekly adjustment is due April 7. "Prices have climbed since the outbreak of the war, but at a much slower pace than overall crude oil." 



  After a long period of deflationary pressure, a bump in price growth could seem welcome, but analysts note if demand remains weak, supply-side inflation will become a headwind. 



  For now, China looks better-placed than most of Asia as it can insulate from the price shock thanks to factors like sizable strategic reserves and the ability to fire up coal capacity if it needs. 



  Citi analysts still project that China's economy will grow 5% this year, expecting little disruption from the Iran war and resilient exports. 





  Japan 



  The Bank of Japan will release its quarterly regional economic report Monday, which investors will look to for hints on the timing of the next interest-rate increase. Signs that wage growth is becoming widespread, for example, could boost tightening expectations. 



  The government is slated to release February household spending data and current account balance figures on Tuesday and Wednesday, respectively. 



  On Wednesday, the BOJ will make outright purchases of three sectors of Japan's government bond market. These include sovereign securities with tenors of more than one year and up to three years and those with tenors of more than 10 years and up to 25. 



  The Ministry of Finance is scheduled to hold two auctions during the week, selling about 600 billion yen of 30-year JGBs on Tuesday and around 2.5 trillion yen of five-year sovereign notes on Thursday. The first could attract more bidding interest from investors due to higher yields which are likely to be offered by the longer-dated securities. 





  Australia/NZ 



  The Reserve Bank of New Zealand is widely expected to hold rates steady on Wednesday, against the backdrop of heightened uncertainty centered around the war in the Middle East. 



  Nomura economists are confident the RBNZ will keep the cash rate at 2.25% next week. "If it publishes a vote count, we think a unanimous decision is most likely," they said. 



  Guidance should be fairly balanced given the geopolitical tensions, though Nomura anticipates  some saber rattling to help contain inflation expectations. 



  Strategists at TD Securities think the central bank will likely reaffirm its reluctance to respond impulsively to the supply shock, especially when the economy is operating below capacity, which should challenge market pricing of more than 75 basis points of hikes this year. 



  Economists at UOB point out that if anything, the RBNZ's previous statement carried a mildly dovish tone, with the projected cash rate ending the year slightly below earlier expectations and peaking at 3.00%. Policymakers will continue to balance inflation trends against the risk of tightening too early when the economic recovery is still fragile, UOB said. 





  Korea 



  On Friday, consensus looks for South Korea's central bank to stand pat as the economy grapples with the uncertainty from the Middle East conflict. 



  Most analysts expect the BOK to keep the policy rate at 2.50%, noting an impending leadership transition at the central bank and the economic risks posed by rising inflation and supply-chain disruptions. 



  Departing BOK Gov. Rhee Chang-yong is likely to maintain a cautious stance rather than offer clear guidance at his final rate-setting meeting on April 10, said Morgan Stanley economist Kathleen Oh. 



  Incoming Gov. Shin Hyun-song is unlikely to give many clues on future policy ahead of his confirmation hearing later this month, Oh said. 



  Higher oil prices pushed inflation above the central bank's 2.0% target in March, posing a headache for South Korea, a major energy importer that relies heavily on oil and gas from the Middle East. 



  ING economist Min Joo Kang expects inflation to accelerate in the coming months on higher energy costs, despite government measures to curb price growth. 



  Kang said the BOK is likely to adopt a wait-and-see stance in April as it assesses whether Middle East-driven energy shocks ease or intensify. 





  India 



  India's central bank rate meets Wednesday as policymakers look to balance growing downside risks to the economy from the Middle East conflict against the threat of an inflationary shock and weakness in the rupee. 



  While macroeconomic fundamentals remain strong, the Indian finance ministry's department of economic affairs has warned of "multi-layered" risks for India due to "its position as a major energy importer with strong trade, investment and remittance linkages" to the Middle East. 



  Tariff risks have eased but other external threats have grown, said DBS economists. Still, they expect the RBI to stand pat as monetary policy will likely be ineffective against the current mix of headwinds. 



  (MORE TO FOLLOW) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 05, 2026 17:14 ET (21:14 GMT)

TTW Reveals Top 100 Convention Centers in the World for 2026

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, April 5, 2026

Travel And Tour World (TTW) reveals global MICE rankings, highlighting top venues driving trade, innovation, and business tourism worldwide.

NEW YORK, April 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ --  Travel And Tour World (TTW) now releases a comprehensive global MICE research report titled TTW Reveals Top 100 Convention Centers in the World for 2026, offering a definitive snapshot of the event venues driving international trade, business tourism, and cross-border economic collaboration. The study captures the rapid evolution of modern meetings, incentives, conferences, and the exhibition industry, where next-generation venues equipped with advanced infrastructure and high-tech digital stages are redefining global gatherings.

These convention centres seamlessly merge the legacy and scale of grand international events with cutting-edge digital innovation, creating immersive, connected, and future-ready environments for global stakeholders.

The report further underscores the accelerating demand for seamless and efficient travel experiences tailored to the needs of today's smart meetings industry travellers. Destinations worldwide are strategically aligning airport infrastructure and hotel infrastructure to enable frictionless mobility, enhanced connectivity, and integrated visitor experiences.

It highlights the critical importance of easy, smart, and sustainable travel solutions for meetings and event industry travellers, event planners, corporate travellers, business tourists, attendees, event delegates, industry leaders, think tanks, and exhibitors. By prioritising accessibility, operational efficiency, and sustainability, these destinations are not only improving delegate experience but also expanding global participation and reinforcing their position within the competitive global MICE landscape.

Global Convention Centres Driving Smart, Sustainable, and Connected Meetings Industry Growth

Across Europe, Asia-Pacific, North America, and the Middle East, mega convention centres are driving a new era of connectivity. These venues combine luxury and comfort with large-scale capacity, positioning themselves as hubs for business exchange and innovation. The rise of bleisure—where business travel merges with leisure experiences—has further reinforced the need for integrated hotel infrastructure, lifestyle amenities, and sustainable urban planning.

The findings reveal that leading destinations are investing heavily in digital tech innovation, hybrid event platforms, and sustainability frameworks. From energy-efficient designs to smart venue management systems, these centres are redefining how industries collaborate in a rapidly evolving global landscape. Sustainability remains central, with green certifications and eco-conscious operations becoming key differentiators.

Moreover, the report positions meetings industry travellers at the heart of this transformation, supported by advanced airport infrastructure, seamless connectivity, and high-tech event ecosystems. These developments not only enhance delegate experience but also strengthen economic resilience and global event expansion.

Anup Kumar Keshan, Founder and Editor-in-Chief of Travel And Tour World notes, "The 2026 report underscores a decisive transformation in the global events industry, driven by sustainability, digital innovation, and economic influence. Nearly 70% of the top 100 venues now hold advanced green certifications, with over 65% targeting net-zero and sustainable operations. Smart venue investments have surged by 40%, led by 5G-enabled hybrid platforms and AI analytics. Industry leaders are redefining engagement through integrated digital ecosystems. Economically, these venues are projected to support over $500 billion in international trade. While Europe and North America remain dominant, rapid expansion across Asia-Pacific and the Middle East highlights shifting global momentum and infrastructure-led growth in smart event ecosystems."

He added, "The findings underscore a decisive shift in the global meetings industry landscape, where exhibition and convention centres are no longer just event venues but strategic drivers of economic growth and international collaboration. As highlighted in the research, leading destinations in the United States, Brazil, Mexico, Canada, Europe, Asia and Africa are leveraging scale, advanced infrastructure, and digital innovation to position themselves at the forefront of global trade and business tourism".

Venue Overview and Strategic Positioning: Infrastructure, Capacity, and Capabilities

1. Messe Frankfurt, Frankfurt, Germany

Messe Frankfurt in Germany stands as one of the world's most influential exhibition and trade fair venues, playing a central role in the global meetings and event  industry. Spanning approximately 590,000 square metres, including over 372,000 sqm of indoor and 60,000 sqm of outdoor space, it offers highly flexible, multi-level halls capable of hosting large-scale concurrent events. The venue regularly attracts 200,000–300,000 attendees, over 5,000 exhibitors, and participants from more than 150 countries, representing industries such as automotive, consumer goods, textiles, and technology.

Flagship events like Ambiente and Automechanika reinforce its global importance, influencing supply chains and innovation trends. Messe Frankfurt combines advanced infrastructure, strong digital capabilities, and sustainability initiatives, including ISO-certified environmental systems. Its strategic location near Frankfurt Airport and integrated transport networks ensures exceptional accessibility, making it a premier global hub for international trade, networking, and industry advancement.

2. Las Vegas Convention Center (LVCC), Las Vegas, USA

LVCC is one of the most technologically advanced convention venues globally, known for hosting CES, the world's leading technology event. Located in Las Vegas, it serves as a global platform for innovation, product launches, and industry networking. Its massive exhibition halls and smart infrastructure enable seamless mega-event hosting. A standout feature is the underground transport system, enhancing internal mobility. The venue supports industries such as consumer electronics, automotive, and construction. Strong connectivity and proximity to hospitality infrastructure make LVCC a cornerstone of global meetings and event industry activities, driving technological advancement and international collaboration.

3. Fiera Milano (Fiera Milano Rho), Milan, Italy

Fiera Milano (Rho), located in Milan, Italy, stands as one of Europe's largest and most influential exhibition and convention centres, playing a vital role in global trade, design, and industrial exchange. The venue spans nearly one million square metres, including over 345,000 sqm of indoor and 60,000 sqm of outdoor exhibition space, supported by 20 modular halls and advanced infrastructure. It regularly hosts major international events such as Salone del Mobile, MICAM, and EICMA, attracting up to 400,000 visitors, 6,000 exhibitors, and participants from over 150 countries. Known for its architectural excellence and efficient layout, it enables simultaneous large-scale events. The venue integrates strong technological systems, sustainability initiatives, and seamless connectivity via rail, metro, and highways. Its proximity to Milan and integration with MiCo Convention Centre further enhance its global standing as a premier MICE destination. 

4. ExCeL London, London, United Kingdom

ExCeL London is one of Europe's most influential exhibition and convention centres, located in the Docklands business district. With over 100,000 sqm of column-free exhibition space, it supports some of the world's largest trade shows and global summits. Hosting up to one million visitors annually, it serves industries including healthcare, finance, and technology. Its modular halls and advanced hybrid event capabilities enable simultaneous mega-events. Strong transport links, including proximity to London City Airport, enhance accessibility. Sustainability initiatives and modern infrastructure reinforce its global stature, positioning London as a dominant meetings and event industry  hub for international business engagement.

5. Georgia International Convention Center (GICC), Atlanta, USA

The Georgia International Convention Center (GICC) is a leading convention facility in Atlanta and one of the few U.S. venues directly connected to an international airport, ensuring exceptional accessibility. It plays a significant role in hosting conventions, trade shows, and corporate events across diverse industries. With flexible meeting spaces, large exhibit halls, and integrated hotel infrastructure, GICC supports mid- to large-scale events with operational efficiency. Its focus on sustainability, modern amenities, and seamless logistics enhances event delivery. Positioned within a major business hub, it contributes to regional economic growth and strengthens the North American MICE ecosystem.

6. Istanbul Congress Center, Istanbul, Turkey

The Istanbul Congress Center is a premier convention venue in Istanbul, strategically positioned at the crossroads of Europe and Asia. It serves as a key hub for international congresses, exhibitions, and corporate events, attracting global delegates across industries. With modern auditoriums, exhibition halls, and flexible meeting spaces, the venue supports large-scale, multi-format events and simultaneous sessions. Its advanced infrastructure and integrated facilities enable efficient event management and high-quality experiences. Located within a major cultural and business district, it plays a vital role in fostering global dialogue, knowledge exchange, and international collaboration within the MICE ecosystem.

7. Singapore Expo Convention & Exhibition Centre, Singapore, Singapore

Singapore Expo is the largest meetings and event industry venue in Singapore, hosting global exhibitions and conferences. Its modular halls and advanced infrastructure enable large-scale event hosting. The venue attracts international participants, reinforcing Singapore's global MICE leadership. Sustainability initiatives enhance its operations. It plays a key role in international trade and business networking.

8. Metro Toronto Convention Centre (MTCC), Toronto, Canada

The Metro Toronto Convention Centre stands as Canada's largest and most prominent convention facility, centrally located in downtown Toronto. It has evolved into a cornerstone of North America's meetings and event industry, hosting global events such as auto shows, expos, and corporate summits. Its expansive exhibition halls and versatile meeting spaces enable simultaneous large-scale events. The venue's accessibility and integration with urban infrastructure enhance its appeal to international delegates. With strong participation across industries like finance, healthcare, and education, MTCC supports Canada's global business presence. Its technological systems and sustainability initiatives further underline its leadership in modern convention management.

9. Paris Nord Villepinte Exhibition Centre, Paris, France

Paris Nord Villepinte Exhibition Centre is one of France's largest and most versatile venues, supporting global exhibitions across food, defence, aviation, and fashion industries. With approximately 246,000 sqm of indoor space and nine modular halls, it accommodates high-capacity events attracting up to 300,000 visitors. Major exhibitions such as SIAL Paris and Eurosatory position it as a key driver of international trade and innovation. Its strategic location near Charles de Gaulle Airport and direct rail connectivity enhances global accessibility. Combined with strong infrastructure, digital systems, and sustainability practices, it plays a vital role in Europe's exhibition ecosystem.

10. Centro Citibanamex, Mexico City, Mexico

Centro Citibanamex serves as Mexico City's premier convention and exhibition venue, playing a central role in Latin America's MICE industry. Its large-scale facilities support international trade shows, corporate events, and industry exhibitions. The venue attracts global corporations and organisations across sectors such as automotive, healthcare, and technology. Its modular halls and advanced infrastructure enable simultaneous events, enhancing operational efficiency. Positioned within a major urban hub, it benefits from strong connectivity and accessibility. The centre contributes significantly to regional economic development by facilitating trade, collaboration, and innovation across Latin America and beyond.

11. Barcelona Fira Gran Via, Barcelona, Spain

Fira Barcelona Gran Via serves as one of Europe's most prominent convention and exhibition venues, playing a pivotal role in Spain's and the broader continent's MICE industry. Its expansive, state-of-the-art facilities host major international trade fairs, congresses, and corporate events across sectors such as technology, healthcare, automotive, and industrial manufacturing. The venue is globally recognised for flagship events like those organised by GSMA, attracting high-level industry leaders and innovators. Its modular, interconnected halls and advanced digital infrastructure allow for large-scale, concurrent events, ensuring seamless operations and flexibility. Strategically located in Barcelona, it benefits from excellent international connectivity and urban accessibility. The venue significantly contributes to regional economic growth by driving business tourism, fostering global trade partnerships, and positioning Barcelona as a leading MICE destination in Europe.

12. International Convention Centre, Sydney, Australia

International Convention Centre Sydney serves as Australia's premier convention, exhibition, and events destination, anchoring the country's presence in the Asia-Pacific MICE industry. The venue hosts a diverse portfolio of international conferences, large-scale exhibitions, and corporate gatherings across sectors such as technology, healthcare, finance, and education. It attracts global organisations, industry leaders, and government delegations, reinforcing its role as a hub for knowledge exchange and business networking. Its flexible design includes expansive exhibition halls, modern theatres, and adaptable meeting spaces that support concurrent, high-capacity events with operational efficiency. Located in Sydney's Darling Harbour, it benefits from strong global connectivity and proximity to hotels, dining, and transport infrastructure. The centre plays a vital role in driving economic growth by boosting business tourism, facilitating trade opportunities, and strengthening Australia's position in the global MICE landscape.

13. São Paulo Expo, São Paulo, Brazil

São Paulo Expo is Brazil's largest convention and exhibition centre, reflecting the country's economic strength and global connectivity. Its expansive facilities support major international trade shows and corporate events across multiple industries. The venue's high-capacity halls and modern design allow simultaneous large-scale exhibitions. It attracts participants from across the Americas, Europe, and Asia, reinforcing São Paulo's role as a leading business hub. Advanced infrastructure and sustainability initiatives ensure efficient operations. By facilitating trade and innovation, São Paulo Expo contributes significantly to Latin America's global economic integration and positions Brazil as a key player in the international MICE industry.

14. Tokyo Big Sight, Tokyo, Japan

Tokyo Big Sight is Japan's largest convention venue, hosting major international exhibitions. Located in Tokyo, it supports industries such as automotive and technology. Its expansive halls enable multi-event hosting. The venue attracts global participants, reinforcing Tokyo's MICE leadership. Sustainability initiatives enhance its operations. Tokyo Big Sight plays a key role in Asia's convention industry.

15. Miami Beach Convention Center (MBCC), Florida, USA

The Miami Beach Convention Center (MBCC) is a premier event venue in Miami Beach, known for hosting major international exhibitions, conventions, and cultural events. Following a significant redevelopment, it features 4 expansive exhibition halls of 491,654 sq. ft, up to 84 meeting rooms, and modern amenities designed for large-scale, multi-event operations. The venue supports diverse industries, attracting global exhibitors and attendees. With advanced infrastructure, integrated technology, and strong sustainability initiatives, MBCC ensures efficient event execution. Located in a globally connected tourism and business destination, it plays a vital role in driving international trade, innovation, and collaboration within the MICE ecosystem.

16. National Exhibition and Convention Center, Shanghai, China

National Exhibition and Convention Center Shanghai stands as one of the world's largest and most advanced exhibition venues, playing a defining role in China's and Asia's MICE industry. The venue hosts large-scale international trade fairs, expos, and corporate events across sectors such as automotive, technology, manufacturing, and consumer goods. It attracts multinational corporations, industry leaders, and government delegations, reinforcing its status as a global business platform. Its vast, interconnected exhibition halls and state-of-the-art infrastructure enable multiple concurrent events with high operational efficiency. Strategically located in Shanghai, it benefits from strong international connectivity and integration with transport networks. The centre contributes significantly to economic growth by facilitating global trade, promoting industrial collaboration, and strengthening Shanghai's position as a leading international business and exhibition hub.

17. India Expo Centre and Mart, Greater Noida, India

India Expo Centre and Mart represents one of South Asia's largest and most versatile exhibition infrastructures, located in the National Capital Region. Spanning approximately 150,000 sqm, it includes over 100,000 sqm of indoor exhibition space supported by modular, column-free halls. The venue accommodates simultaneous high-capacity events, drawing up to 200,000 attendees and thousands of exhibitors globally. It serves diverse sectors including automotive, IT, manufacturing, and agriculture. Equipped with advanced digital infrastructure and sustainable systems, the centre enhances India's role in global trade ecosystems. Its proximity to Delhi and strong transport connectivity further strengthen its positioning as a strategic MICE gateway in the region.

18. Messe Berlin, Berlin, Germany

Messe Berlin is a leading international exhibition and convention venue in Berlin, renowned for hosting major global trade fairs and industry events. It features extensive 40 exhibition halls and events areas, and flexible meeting spaces designed for large-scale and concurrent events. The venue supports diverse sectors, attracting international exhibitors, businesses, and policymakers. With advanced infrastructure, strong digital capabilities, and efficient logistics, it enables seamless event execution. Strategically located in a major European capital with excellent connectivity, Messe Berlin plays a crucial role in driving global trade, innovation, and cross-industry collaboration within the MICE ecosystem.

19. National Exhibition Centre, Birmingham, UK

National Exhibition Centre Birmingham serves as the United Kingdom's largest and most prominent exhibition venue, playing a central role in Europe's MICE industry. The venue hosts a wide range of international trade shows, consumer exhibitions, and corporate events across sectors such as manufacturing, automotive, retail, and technology. It attracts global businesses, industry professionals, and large public audiences, reinforcing its status as a key platform for commerce and networking. Its extensive, flexible exhibition halls and modern infrastructure enable the seamless delivery of large-scale and concurrent events with high operational efficiency. Strategically located in Birmingham, it benefits from excellent connectivity, including proximity to Birmingham Airport and major rail and road networks. The centre significantly contributes to regional and national economic growth by driving business tourism, supporting trade, and enhancing the UK's global events landscape.

20. La Rural, Buenos Aires, Argentina

La Rural serves as one of Argentina's leading convention and exhibition venues, playing a key role in Latin America's MICE industry. Located in Buenos Aires, the venue hosts a diverse range of international trade fairs, corporate events, and cultural exhibitions across sectors such as agriculture, livestock, technology, and consumer industries. It attracts global exhibitors, industry professionals, and large public audiences, reinforcing its position as a major business and networking hub. Its flexible pavilions, outdoor spaces, and modern event infrastructure enable the seamless organisation of large-scale and concurrent events. Strategically positioned within a vibrant urban district, it benefits from strong accessibility and proximity to hotels and transport networks. The centre contributes significantly to economic growth by promoting trade, fostering industry collaboration, and strengthening Buenos Aires' role in the regional and global MICE landscape.

21. COEX Convention and Exhibition Center

COEX Convention and Exhibition Center serves as South Korea's premier convention and exhibition hub, playing a vital role in Asia's MICE industry. Located in Seoul, the venue hosts major international conferences, trade fairs, and corporate events across sectors such as technology, finance, healthcare, and entertainment. It attracts global corporations, startups, and industry leaders, reinforcing its status as a key platform for innovation and business exchange. Its modern, flexible exhibition halls, conference rooms, and integrated facilities support large-scale and concurrent events with high operational efficiency. Positioned within the Gangnam district, it benefits from excellent connectivity, direct access to public transport, and proximity to hotels, retail, and entertainment complexes. The centre significantly contributes to economic growth by driving business tourism, fostering global partnerships, and strengthening Seoul's position as a leading international MICE destination.

22. Orange County Convention Center (OCCC), Orlando, United States

The Orange County Convention Center (OCCC) stands as one of the largest convention facilities in the United States, positioned strategically in Orlando's thriving tourism and business corridor. It is widely recognised for hosting globally influential events across healthcare, simulation, and entertainment sectors. With more than 185,000 square metres of exhibition space and over 100 meeting rooms, it enables simultaneous mega-events within its interconnected campus. The venue supports high visitor volumes exceeding 100,000 attendees and attracts multinational corporations like Microsoft and Siemens. Its certified infrastructure, advanced digital systems, and proximity to Orlando International Airport make it a premier destination for global MICE events.

23. Indonesia Convention Exhibition (ICE), BSD City, Indonesia

ICE is Indonesia's largest convention venue, hosting international exhibitions and conferences. Located near Jakarta, it supports industries such as manufacturing and healthcare. Its modular halls enable large-scale event hosting. The venue attracts global participants, strengthening Indonesia's MICE industry. Sustainability initiatives enhance its operations. ICE plays a key role in Southeast Asia's convention landscape.

24. Vancouver Convention Centre, Vancouver, Canada

The Vancouver Convention Centre is widely recognised for combining architectural excellence with sustainability leadership. Located along the waterfront, it offers a distinctive event environment with scenic appeal. Its Platinum certification highlights its commitment to eco-friendly operations, including seawater heating and green roofs. The venue hosts global conferences, trade shows, and policy forums, reinforcing Vancouver's international relevance. Its flexible halls and advanced infrastructure support diverse event formats, including hybrid and large-scale exhibitions. By attracting participants from across continents, it strengthens Canada's global MICE footprint while setting benchmarks for sustainable convention centre design and operations.

25. Seattle Convention Center (SCC), Seattle, United States

The Seattle Convention Center represents a modern evolution in urban convention infrastructure, emerging as a flagship venue aligned with Seattle's technology-driven economy. Its expansion with the Summit building significantly increased its exhibition footprint, enabling large-scale, column-free event hosting. The venue supports industries such as technology, healthcare, and biotechnology, attracting global corporations and innovators. With high-speed connectivity, certified sustainability standards, and seamless integration into downtown Seattle, SCC reflects the future of smart convention centres. Its ability to host tens of thousands of attendees reinforces its importance as a hub for innovation, networking, and global collaboration in North America.

26. Messe Düsseldorf, Düsseldorf, Germany

Messe Düsseldorf is a globally recognised hub for industrial, medical, and technological exhibitions, playing a central role in Europe's business landscape. Offering over 305,000 sqm of indoor space across 17 halls, it is designed for heavy-duty industrial events with advanced logistics capabilities. Hosting leading events such as MEDICA and K Fair, the venue attracts up to 250,000 visitors and thousands of exhibitors from more than 150 countries. Its proximity to Düsseldorf Airport enhances accessibility, while its smart infrastructure and sustainability initiatives ensure operational excellence. Messe Düsseldorf remains a benchmark venue for specialised, high-value global trade fairs.

27. Istanbul Expo Center, Istanbul, Turkey

Istanbul Expo Center is a major MICE hub bridging Europe and Asia, located near Istanbul's international airport. With over 100,000 sqm of exhibition space, it supports large-scale trade fairs and conferences. Hosting over one million visitors annually, it serves industries such as automotive, construction, and textiles. Its modular halls and advanced infrastructure allow simultaneous events. Strong connectivity and proximity to airports enhance global accessibility. Sustainability practices and digital capabilities strengthen its competitiveness. The venue positions Istanbul as a strategic global gateway for trade, innovation, and business networking across continents.

28. Riocentro, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

Riocentro is one of Latin America's largest convention and exhibition complexes, located in Rio de Janeiro. It hosts major international trade fairs, congresses, and large-scale events across diverse industries. The venue features 4 interconnected pavilions with a capacity of 7000 to 16750 people, expansive exhibition areas with multiple exits, and flexible meeting spaces designed for simultaneous events. With robust infrastructure, integrated services, and high-capacity operations, it accommodates large visitor and exhibitor volumes efficiently. Positioned in a globally recognized tourism and business destination, Riocentro plays a key role in driving regional economic activity, international trade, and cross-industry collaboration within the MICE ecosystem.

29. McCormick Place, Chicago, United States

McCormick Place in Chicago is the largest convention centre in North America, serving as a premier destination for global trade shows and conventions. Spanning over 260,000 sqm of exhibition space within a 600,000 sqm complex, it features four interconnected buildings and over 170 meeting rooms. Jio World Convention Centre serves as India's premier convention and exhibition venue, playing a vital role in the country's rapidly expanding MICE industry. Located in Mumbai, the centre hosts high-profile international conferences, exhibitions, and corporate events across sectors such as finance, technology, entertainment, and global trade. It attracts multinational corporations, industry leaders, and policymakers, reinforcing Mumbai's status as a key business hub. Its state-of-the-art infrastructure, including large convention halls, ballrooms, and meeting spaces, supports seamless execution of large-scale and concurrent events. Strategically positioned within a major commercial district, it benefits from strong connectivity and premium hospitality access. The centre significantly contributes to economic growth by driving business tourism, fostering global partnerships, and strengthening India's presence in the global MICE landscape.

87. Bahrain Exhibition World (BEW), Manama, Bahrain

BEW is Bahrain's flagship convention venue, hosting international exhibitions and conferences. Located in Manama, it supports industries such as finance and energy. Its modular halls and advanced infrastructure enable large-scale event hosting. The venue attracts global participants, strengthening Bahrain's MICE industry. Sustainability initiatives enhance its operations. BEW plays a key role in the Gulf region's convention sector.

88. Taipei Nangang Exhibition Center, Taipei, Taiwan

Taipei Nangang Exhibition Center represents Taiwan's premier MICE facility, delivering over 100,000 sqm of exhibition space across modular halls. Hosting global events like Computex, it attracts up to one million visitors annually, supporting technology-driven industries with advanced infrastructure, sustainability initiatives, and seamless urban connectivity in East Asia.

89. Jeju Convention Center

Jeju International Convention Center combines scenic island surroundings with 15,000 sqm exhibition space. Hosting tourism, renewable energy, and corporate events, it attracts regional and global audiences while promoting sustainable practices and integrating leisure with business, positioning Jeju as a distinctive MICE destination in East Asia.

90. AsiaWorld Expo

AsiaWorld-Expo serves as a leading convention and exhibition hub in Asia, playing a vital role in the region's MICE industry. Located in Hong Kong adjacent to the international airport, it hosts major global trade shows, exhibitions, concerts, and corporate events across sectors such as aviation, technology, logistics, and entertainment. The venue attracts international businesses and industry leaders, reinforcing Hong Kong's position as a global events destination. Its modern, flexible halls and strong connectivity enable efficient large-scale events while driving business tourism and economic growth.

91. Macau Venetian Convention and Exhibition Centre

The Venetian Macao Convention and Exhibition Centre serves as one of Asia's largest integrated convention and exhibition venues, playing a significant role in the region's MICE industry. Located in Macau, it hosts international trade shows, corporate events, and exhibitions across sectors such as hospitality, gaming, technology, and retail. The venue attracts global businesses and industry leaders, reinforcing Macau's position as a key events destination. Its expansive halls and integrated resort infrastructure enable efficient large-scale events while driving business tourism and regional economic growth.

92. Suntec Singapore Convention & Exhibition Centre, Singapore

Suntec Singapore Convention Centre is a leading Southeast Asian MICE venue offering 42,000 sqm of flexible space. Hosting over one million attendees annually, it supports industries like finance and technology, combining central connectivity, advanced hybrid event capabilities, and sustainable operations within Singapore's global business ecosystem.

93. Oman Convention & Exhibition Centre (OCEC), Muscat, Oman

OCEC is Oman's flagship convention venue, designed for large-scale international events. Located in Muscat, it supports industries such as energy, tourism, and healthcare. Its modern infrastructure and flexible halls enable diverse event formats. The venue attracts regional and global participants, strengthening Oman's MICE industry. Sustainability initiatives and advanced technology enhance its capabilities. OCEC plays a key role in positioning Muscat as a business and networking hub in the Gulf region.

94. Osaka International Convention Center (Grand Cube Osaka), Japan

Grand Cube Osaka is a central Japanese MICE hub offering 10,000 sqm of exhibition space and hosting international conferences across healthcare, technology, and academia. Its modern infrastructure, strong connectivity, and multi-purpose facilities support knowledge exchange, reinforcing Osaka's position as a leading business and innovation centre in Asia.

95. Corferias – Bogotá International Conference & Exhibition Center, Colombia

Corferias in Bogotá offers 70,000 sqm of exhibition space and hosts major trade fairs like AgroExpo. Serving multiple industries, it attracts large international participation, supporting Colombia's economic sectors and reinforcing Bogotá's position as a leading MICE destination in Latin America with strong infrastructure and global connectivity.

96. Wisconsin Center (Baird Center), Milwaukee, United States

The Baird Center in Milwaukee serves as a key regional convention hub, supporting industries such as manufacturing, healthcare, and education. Its flexible, column-free halls and modular layouts allow efficient hosting of trade shows and conferences. Located in downtown Milwaukee, it benefits from strong urban connectivity, linking visitors to hotels and business districts. The venue is known for operational efficiency and mid-to-large-scale event capability. Sustainability initiatives, including energy-efficient systems, further enhance its appeal. While primarily regional, its role in facilitating industry networking and business development makes it a critical Midwest MICE destination.

97. Panama Convention Center, Panama City, Panama

Panama Convention Center is Central America's largest venue, offering 25,000 sqm exhibition space. Hosting logistics, finance, and technology events, it supports regional and global business engagement, leveraging Panama City's strategic location and connectivity to strengthen its role as a gateway for trade and conferences across the Americas.

98. Lima Convention Center, Lima, Peru

Lima Convention Center delivers 15,000 sqm of flexible space, hosting mining, finance, and technology events. Positioned in Peru's capital, it supports international conferences and trade shows, combining modern infrastructure and connectivity to enhance Lima's role as a key MICE and business destination in South America.

99. Palexpo, Geneva, Switzerland

Palexpo is Switzerland's largest exhibition and convention centre, strategically located in Geneva next to the international airport, ensuring seamless global accessibility. It hosts major international trade shows and congresses, particularly in sectors like mobility, aviation, and luxury industries. With over 100,000 square metres of flexible, modular space, it supports large-scale exhibitions, corporate events, and concurrent gatherings. The venue features advanced infrastructure, efficient logistics, and strong digital capabilities. Emphasising sustainability and operational excellence, Palexpo plays a vital role in facilitating global business exchange, innovation, and cross-industry collaboration within the European and international MICE ecosystem.

100. Santiago Espacio Riesco, Santiago, Chile

Espacio Riesco serves as one of Chile's leading convention and exhibition venues, playing a key role in Latin America's MICE industry. Located in Santiago, the venue hosts major international trade fairs, exhibitions, and corporate events across sectors such as mining, agriculture, technology, and consumer industries. It offers over 120,000+ sqm of total event space, including indoor halls and outdoor areas, enabling large-scale and concurrent events with high operational efficiency. The venue attracts global exhibitors, industry leaders, and regional stakeholders, reinforcing Santiago's position as a strategic business hub. With strong connectivity and modern infrastructure, it contributes significantly to economic growth by driving business tourism, promoting trade, and fostering industry collaboration.

"The rise of mega venues across the Germany, UK, Spain, United States, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa, alongside Europe's continued leadership and North America's technological edge, reflects a highly competitive and rapidly evolving ecosystem. What stands out is the growing emphasis on sustainability, connectivity, and hybrid event capabilities, which are now essential benchmarks for global competitiveness. These developments indicate that the future of the MICE industry will be shaped by venues that can seamlessly integrate technology, accommodate large-scale participation, and support sustainable operations, ultimately redefining how global industries connect, collaborate, and drive long-term economic value", says, Anup Kumar Keshan, Founder and Editor-in-Chief, Travel And Tour World.

Research Methodology

The research methodology for this study on global MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, and Exhibitions) venues is based on a structured, multi-layered approach combining secondary data analysis, editorial insights and feedbacks from 25 million readers, comparative benchmarking, and qualitative assessment of infrastructure and industry positioning.

1. Research Design

This study adopts a descriptive and comparative research design, aimed at analysing and benchmarking the world's leading exhibition and convention centres across regions including Europe, Asia-Pacific, North America, the Middle East, Africa, and Latin America. The objective is to identify patterns in scale, infrastructure, global importance, and industry recognition among top-performing venues.

2. Data Collection Method

The research is primarily based on data sources, compiled from industry reports, official venue documentation, global MICE rankings. The dataset includes 100 major exhibition and convention venues worldwide, with detailed profiling of selected flagship venues such as Messegelände Hannover, National Exhibition and Convention Center Shanghai, Messe Frankfurt, and others.

3. Data Parameters and Variables

The study evaluates each venue based on consistent analytical variables, including:

  • Venue size (indoor and outdoor exhibition space)
  • Infrastructure capabilities (modular halls, logistics systems, meeting facilities)
  • Event scale (attendees, exhibitors, global participation)
  • Key events hosted and their global significance
  • Technological integration (digital infrastructure, hybrid capabilities)
  • Sustainability initiatives (energy efficiency, certifications)
  • Connectivity (proximity to airports, rail, and urban transport systems)

These parameters allow for a standardised comparison across diverse global venues.

5. Analytical Approach

The research employs a comparative benchmarking framework, where venues are analysed against each other to identify:

  • Leaders in scale and capacity
  • Regional strengths (e.g., Europe in industrial trade fairs, Asia in mega-integrated venues)
  • Emerging trends such as sustainability, smart infrastructure, and hybrid event capabilities
  • Feedbacks from more 25 million business delegates, MICE Leaders, business travellers, corporate travellers

A qualitative synthesis approach is used to interpret how these venues contribute to global trade, innovation, and business tourism.

6. Case Study Integration

Detailed case study analysis has been conducted for selected high-impact venues, including:

  • Messegelände Hannover (Germany)
  • National Exhibition and Convention Center Shanghai (China)
  • Messe Frankfurt (Germany)
  • Fiera Milano (Italy) and more convention centres

These case studies provide deeper insights into operational scale, technological advancements, and strategic importance within the global MICE ecosystem.

7. Validation and Reliability

To ensure accuracy and reliability:

  • Data points were cross-referenced with multiple industry sources
  • Consistent metrics were applied across all venues

This strengthens the credibility of the comparative analysis.

About Travel And Tour World (TTW)

Travel and Tour World (TTW) is a premier global digital travel media platform reaching over 25 million readers worldwide across 104 regional platforms in European, Asian, African, Middle East and Indian languages. Since 2009, TTW has been a trusted source of travel and tourism industry news, intelligence, and trend analysis. Through insightful content, reliable information, and effective communication, TTW delivers valuable perspectives that support informed decision-making and inspire modern travellers worldwide. Built on years of industry expertise, the platform has evolved into a trusted source for comprehensive travel and tourism insights.

As an official media partner to more than 1,500 international travel and hospitality events, including WTM Events, ITB Asia, ITB China, IMEX America & Frankfurt, and ATM, TTW connects tourism boards, airlines, hotels, tour operators, MICE planners, and luxury travel brands with global industry decision-makers. With its unmatched multilingual reach and editorial credibility, TTW continues to shape conversations that define the future of global travel.

Details of the case: According to the lawsuit, defendants provided investors with material information concerning their expected results for the Phase 3 Orbit and COSMIC studies for setrusumab in Osteogenesis Imperfecta (OI). Defendants' statements included, among other things, confidence in setrusumab's ability to ultimately reduce the annualized fracture rates of the tested patients and in the study itself to put setrusumab in an opportunity to succeed in reaching statistical significance of this key endpoint.
